title: The Exegesis of Philip K. Dick — Page 91
raw_text:
as the rotting fruit is to the growing seed within; as the fruit rots, the seed
within grows; a double motion within the single entity: the outer toward
death, the inner toward life. What grows within me grows perhaps a new body
as well as a new spirit, and discards both of the outer ones together.
work_title: The Exegesis of Philip K. Dick
